qa_pairs.question: What are the different layers of abstraction in the I/O Software and Hardware stack used for organizing parallel I/O in HPC systems?
qa_pairs.answer: The I/O Software and Hardware stack for parallel I/O consists of several layers of abstraction, from bottom to top: I/O Hardware, Parallel filesystem, I/O Middleware, High-end I/O library, and finally the Application.

qa_pairs.question: What is the foundational layer of the parallel I/O abstraction stack, and what does it comprise?
qa_pairs.answer: The foundational layer of the parallel I/O abstraction stack is the I/O hardware, which consists of a physical array or hard disks attached to the cluster.
